fact_nuggets.text: Sharpness (Japanese: Sharpness) is an Ability introduced in Generation IX.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Affected moves 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Sharpness boosts the strength of slicing moves used by a Pokemon with this Ability by 50%. Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Sharpness, as well as the Pokemon that can have Sharpness and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Power and Boost Learned by Aerial Ace Flying Physical 60 90 Air Cutter Flying Special 60 90 Air Slash Flying Special 75 112.5 Aqua Cutter Water Physical 70 105 Ceaseless Edge Dark Physical 65 97.5 Fury Cutter Bug Physical 40 60 Leaf Blade Grass Physical 90 135 Night Slash Dark Physical 70 105 Psycho Cut Psychic Physical 70 105 Razor Shell Water Physical 75 112.5 Sacred Sword Fighting Physical 90 135 Slash Normal Physical 70 105 Solar Blade Grass Physical 125 187.5 Stone Axe Rock Physical 65 97.5 X-Scissor Bug Physical 80 120 Although the following moves are affected by the Ability, they currently cannot be learned by any Pokemon with Sharpness. They may, however, be called via Metronome , Copycat , or Mimic , or be used by a Pokemon that gained Sharpness by an Ability like Trace or Receiver or a move such as Skill Swap or Role Play . Move Type Cat. Power and Boost Behemoth Blade Steel Physical 100 150 Bitter Blade Fire Physical 90 135 Cross Poison Poison Physical 70 105 Cut Normal Physical 50 75 Kowtow Cleave Dark Physical 85 127.5 Mighty Cleave Rock Physical 95 142.5 Population Bomb Normal Physical 20 30 Psyblade Psychic Physical 80 120 Razor Leaf Grass Physical 55 82.5 Secret Sword Fighting Special 85 127.5 Tachyon Cutter Steel Special 50 75 Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Sharpness has no effect outside of battle.
